notes: Commercial RISC/Unix market peaked mid-2000s; replaced by x86-64 Linux servers
PA-8000 launched 1996; PA-RISC line ended with PA-8900 around 2008; HP moved to Itanium
HP-UX reached end of standard support December 31 2025
RS/6000 rebranded as pSeries then IBM Power Systems; IBM Power architecture still active
AIX remains active on IBM Power Systems as of 2026
SPARC development ended 2017; Fujitsu continued M-series SPARC until ~2022
Java became one of most widely used programming languages globally
Itanium released 2001; last Itanium processor shipped 2017; Intel discontinued in 2021
Evolved into Windows 2000 Server and subsequent Windows Server releases
ERP on RISC/Unix was key use case; most ERP now runs on Linux/x86
Relational databases evolved; Oracle dominant; Informix acquired by IBM 2001
NUMA became standard in multi-socket x86 and RISC servers
RS/6000 SP evolved into IBM eServer pSeries 690; eventually replaced by IBM Power servers
DataBlade technology absorbed into IBM after Informix acquisition in 2001
OLTP remains a core enterprise workload category
Data warehousing evolved into cloud data warehouses; Snowflake, BigQuery etc dominant by 2020s
